summary: Verified OTP dev-fallback bug fix end-to-end. Backend: 6/6 relevant pytest cases passed (1 skipped for optional admin coupons endpoint). Frontend: signup -> /verify-email -> dev banner + prefilled 6-digit inputs -> wrong code shows error banner -> correct code verifies + navigates to /dashboard. Regression checks (admin login, /api/offers list w/ brand_url, unverified claim=403) all pass.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1395 lines - consider splitting into routers (auth, offers, scan, admin) for maintainability.
DEV_OTP_FALLBACK is read at import time; ensure it is explicitly set to 'false' in production .env to avoid leaking OTPs in API responses.
Consider masking dev_otp in server logs (currently logger.info prints full OTP).
test_credentials: admin@savycampusdeals.in / Admin@123 (verified working). Test student emails were dynamically generated (TEST_<uuid>@example.com).
seed_data_creation: Created 3 disposable @example.com test users via /api/auth/register; one verified via dev_otp.
context_for_next_testing_agent: DEV_OTP_FALLBACK=true is set in backend/.env. Because Resend key is in TEST mode, every non-owner recipient returns email_sent:false and includes dev_otp in the register/send-otp response body. If Resend is ever upgraded (verified domain), dev_otp should DISAPPEAR from responses and banner should not render.
rca of the issue: Resend API in TEST mode raises for non-owner recipients. Previous send_email() raised/logged and register still succeeded but user had no way to obtain OTP. Fix converts send_email into a result dict, and when DEV_OTP_FALLBACK=true and delivery fails, register/send-otp return {email_sent:false, dev_otp, email_error}. Frontend VerifyEmailOtp reads dev_otp from router state / resend response, auto-fills inputs and shows amber dev banner. Verified working end-to-end.
